post.summary: Login using username and password. Will return a JWT when logging in was successful.

200.description:
Login was successful. A jwt-token will only be returned for users with verified mail addresses.
            If a mail address is not verified, the response object property ErrorState will be `mail_not_verified`.

get.summary: Get a user by its e-mail address
Get a user by its id

newPassword.description: Gets or sets the new password

ChangePasswordRequest.description:
Model for requesting a password change. When requesting a password change,
a verification code is required

tokenId.description: Gets or sets the token to verify

token.description: Gets or sets the produced token (e.g. jwt token or two-factor token)

errorState.description:
Gets or sets the actual error state. Will be set to `mail_not_verified` if the login
was successful, but the mail address is not verified.

tokenType.description:
Gets or sets the produced token.
<list type="bullet">
`jwt`: Login completed, no two-factor required
`two-factor`: A second two-factor auth is required
Empty when mail address is not verified.
</list>

id.description: Gets or sets the id of the requested user

eMail.description: Gets or sets the mail address for the request user

userName.description: Gets or sets the name for the requested user.

RequestUserResponse.description: Result of a successfully requested user

organizationId.description: Gets or sets the organization id to generate a jwt for

SelectOrganizationRequest.description: Request for changing/selecting a specific organization

email.description: Gets or sets the mail-address to verify

tokenId.description: Gets or sets the token to verify

code.description: Gets or sets the verification code

email.description: Gets or sets the mail-address to verify

code.description: Gets or sets the verification code

Bearer.description:
JWT Authorization header using the Bearer scheme. 

 Enter 'Bearer' [space] and then your token in the text input below.

Example: "Bearer 12345abcdef"
